sections.title: Performa Analysis ‚Äî Rekab Kelas
1. N+1 Query Patterns
üî¥ Admin Stats ‚Äî `perKelas` loop
üî¥ Attendance POST ‚Äî per-student in a loop
2. Client-Side Data Fetching (No Cache, No Cleanup)
üî¥ Raw `fetch` in `useEffect` everywhere
3. Redundant Computations
üî¥ Stats route ‚Äî 5 separate `.filter()` calls on the same array
üü° Login route ‚Äî 3 sequential `findUnique` calls
4. React Re-Render Issues
üî¥ Inline arrow functions in `.map()`
üü° Stat cards recreated on every render
üü° Massive single component (Recap page)
sections.content:
**File:** `src/app/recap/page.tsx` ‚Äî **460+ lines** of JSX with 14+ `useState` hooks

**Problem:** Every state change re-renders the entire tree. The bulanan tab and harian tab are mutually exclusive but share state, so changing a harian filter re-renders the bulanan DOM too.

**Fix ‚Äî split into smaller components:**
- `DailyRecapSection` ‚Äî owns harian state
- `MonthlyRecapSection` ‚Äî owns bulanan state
- `PhotoModal` ‚Äî separate modal component

Then the parent only decides which tab to show, no shared state pollution:

```typescript
{tab === 'bulanan' ? <MonthlyRecapSection /> : <DailyRecapSection />}
```

---

sections.title: 5. Memory Leaks
üî¥ No AbortController on any useEffect fetch
sections.content: **All client pages** are affected. Switching between tabs, changing filters, or unmounting mid-fetch leaves the fetch promise hanging. It resolves, sets state on an unmounted component ‚Äî React logs warnings in dev mode, and in production the callback queue holds references preventing GC.

sections.title: üü° In-memory rate limit store
6. Server-Side Caching Opportunities
üî¥ `force-dynamic` on every route prevents all response caching
üü° File uploads saved to disk
sections.content:
**Files saved to `public/uploads/` with no cleanup** ‚Äî over time this grows without bound. No dedup for identical uploads, no cleanup for abandoned uploads (student submits permit but the record is deleted).

---

sections.title: 7. Bundle / Network
üü° jspdf dynamic import nesting
üü° Duplicated `exportDailyPDF` function
sections.content:
**`recap/page.tsx` and `recap/public/page.tsx`** both define the same ~40-line function. Aside from maintenance debt, it's shipped twice in the client bundle.

**Fix:** Extract to `@/utils/pdfExport.ts` as already done for monthly PDF.

---

sections.title: Priority Summary
sections.content:
| Severity | Issue | Effort | Impact |
|----------|-------|--------|--------|
| **CRITICAL** | N+1 in attendance POST handler | Low (add batch queries) | High for large class sizes |
| **CRITICAL** | No AbortController anywhere | Low (wrap in custom hook) | Race conditions + memory leaks |
| **HIGH** | N+1 in stats perKelas | Medium | Slow dashboard load with many classes |
| **HIGH** | No caching on any API route | Low (set Cache-Control headers) | Unnecessary DB hits |
| **HIGH** | Redundant `.filter()` loops | Low (single pass) | Small gain per call, accumulates |
| **MEDIUM** | Massive single component (recap) | Medium (split components) | Smoother tab switching |
| **MEDIUM** | Sequential login queries | Low (Promise.all) | ~30ms faster login |
| **MEDIUM** | In-memory rate limiter leaks | Low (add cleanup interval) | Prevents OOM under many IPs |
| **LOW** | Inline arrow functions in map | Low | Prepares for memoization |
| **LOW** | jspdf sequential import | Low | PDF export loads faster |

---

sections.title: Quickest Wins (implement in order)
sections.content:
1. **Add `AbortController` to all `useEffect` fetches** ‚Äî shared `useFetch` hook, ~15 min
2. **Batch attendance POST queries** ‚Äî `findMany` + `Map` instead of loop, ~10 min
3. **`Cache-Control` headers on stable routes** ‚Äî `/api/classes`, `/api/recap/daily/status`, ~5 min
4. **Single-pass filter in stats/recap routes** ‚Äî `reduce` or `forEach` instead of 5x `.filter`, ~5 min
5. **`Promise.all` on login queries** ‚Äî ~2 min
6. **Extract duplicated `exportDailyPDF`** ‚Äî reduce bundle size, ~5 min
